key_insight: A transfer-learning PINN can reuse learned electrochemical physics to estimate lithium-ion battery states with SPMe while reducing retraining cost across battery conditions.
engineering_relevance: This offers a faster, physics-consistent surrogate for battery diagnostics and control, useful when full numerical electrochemical simulation is too expensive.
method: Pretrain a PINN on general SPMe electrochemical dynamics.
Transfer weights to the target battery, freeze selected layers, and fine-tune the rest.
Estimate key state variables while enforcing conservation laws in the loss.
evidence_signal: Validation with PyBaMM shows accurate voltage prediction and improved training efficiency for the transfer-learning SPMe-PINN.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the pretrained source battery matches the target chemistry and operating regime.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Network with Transfer Learning for State Estimation in Lithium-Ion Batteries using the Single Particle Model with Electrolyte
